DT Swiss ARC 1100 Dicut 55 review: a polished performer, but look elsewhere for more progressive or better-value options
description: Although fast and well-made, the design is conservative and there are better-value buys out there

component_name: Garmin Edge 550's fiddly interface and high asking price stop it being a winner
description: The Garmin Edge 550 has a bright screen that increases readability, but the button-only interface is fiddly to use

component_name: Brembo's GR-PRO is braking's worst-kept secret, but deserves your attention
description: Brembo has built some of the most adjustable brakes on the market, with buckets of stopping power. But you'll have to dig deep to pay for them

component_name: Pademelon Steering Damper delivers calmer, more stable handling, but can you justify the price?
description: A pricey upgrade for those looking for marginal but meaningful gains

component_name: Oquo's RP50 LTD wheelset is light enough for the road and tough enough for gravel
description: A high-grade wheelset with looks to match the superb ride

component_name: Schmolke's TLO 50 wheels are the lightest I’ve ever tested – and among the best, too
description: Headline-grabbing lightweight wheels that roll brilliantly on the road

component_name: Forget the big brands, Microshift Advent MX 11-speed is the workhorse drivetrain your ebike needs
description: Advent MX hits a very convincing sweet spot for riders who prioritise reliable gearing over cutting-edge complexity

component_name: Ekoi’s PW8 is the best clipless pedal system I’ve tried, but there’s a catch
description: Superb clipless pedal alternative to the category leaders, but it desperately needs more shoe options

component_name: Vittoria’s Rubino V Tubeless tyre is the best Rubino I’ve tried, but it struggles in the wet
description: The latest Vittoria Rubino is an appealing training tyre

component_name: The Lewis LHT Ultimate brakes have tons of power, but I'll be sticking with mainstream brands for two reasons
description: The Lewis brakes have massive fork-bending power and bling looks, but they're tricky to bleed and the wandering bite point was an issue

component_name: SRAM Motive Ultimate Expert brakes provide solid stopping but lack outright power for tougher trails
description: Beautifully light lever feel and easy-to-control power make this a great option for cross-country and trail bikes. They lack the punch of the outgoing Codes, though

component_name: Boost's Brompton ebike conversion kit is the lightweight, affordable route to an electric Brompton
description: The Boost kit is a winner for electrifying your Brompton – it's lighter, cheaper and more practical than rival options

component_name: SRAM Eagle Powertrain electric bike motor
description: Powertrain takes the mundanity out of riding, leaving you to focus on the extraordinary. But it’s not perfect

component_name: RockShox ZEB Ultimate Charger 3.1 is the enduro fork to beat
description: Price, performance, weight and tuneability make the Charger 3.1 ZEB the new long-travel single-crown fork benchmark

component_name: Fox Podium Factory offers unrivalled performance, but the price makes me feel dizzy
description: Bar the lofty price and weight, it’s impossible to say a bad thing about the Podium’s excellent performance

component_name: The Cane Creek Invert CS is all the gravel fork you need – just don't mention the price
description: A lightweight, minimal suspension fork that flows smoothly and maintains stiffness on rough surfaces

component_name: SRAM XX SL Eagle AXS Transmission is equally at home on my enduro bike and my race bike
description: Excellent shifting and durability, but very expensive

component_name: SRAM Eagle 90 Transmission is the best mechanical drivetrain yet, but Shimano does one thing better
description: Sturdy, reliable shifting for analogue lifers

component_name: New SRAM Rival AXS leaves Shimano 105 Di2 trailing in its wake
description: New Rival AXS improves every element – it’s now the mid-range groupset to beat

component_name: The Redshift Shockstop suspension stem offers excellent comfort and fit options, but adjustment isn't as easy as the competition
description: The Redshift ShockStop is stiff yet smoothing stem with a wide range of size options

component_name: USE's Vybe GR gravel stem performs best when you're in the drops
description: A lightweight, active comfort-providing addition to any gravel bike
The Traction Lock-On Aramid grips provide good traction in all conditions, but the dual-clamp design won’t suit everyone

component_name: Outlier Pendulum flat pedals offer unrivalled grip, but one flaw might put you off
description: Brilliant grip and stability with a truly innovative design, but the large bearing bulge interfered way too much with foot placement

component_name: Shimano M540 MTB SPD pedals - two sided mechanism
description: If you want a dependable and affordable SPD pedal that will last your entire riding career – and beyond – don’t bother with anything else, just buy these

component_name: 8 Degree Pedal X8-2 Floating Pedals cure joint pain, but there's a reason I can't recommend them
description: Design limitations cause problems that dampen the impressive floating platform performance

component_name: The RockShox Super Deluxe Ultimate is simply the best rear shock you can buy
description: The perfect mix of smoothness and support makes the Super Deluxe Ultimate the best rear shock available, as long as the tune works for you and your bike

component_name: RockShox Vivid Ultimate's trail-smoothing traction and usable adjustability equal impressive performance
description: A highly adaptable, adjustable and plush-feeling gravity-based rear shock
A top performer for harder riding and going big

component_name: Specialized Power Comp with MIMIC Saddle
description: Supportive saddle that offers excellent comfort for short to mid-length rides and doesn’t hinder descending

component_name: Ergon SM E-Mountain Sport Women Saddle
description: Great for riders who spend a lot of time in the saddle and aren’t getting super-gnarly on the tech, but the high back could be an issue
An all-rounder saddle that provides comfort on and off-road, but manoeuvrability is hindered on technical trails

component_name: Fox Transfer Factory Neo dropper seatpost offers top-level performance, but can you afford it?
description: Brilliant performance, but it comes at a significant cost

component_name: New RockShox Reverb AXS B1 dropper post is so good I’m going to buy one
description: More affordable, light and reliable, the Reverb AXS dropper sets the benchmark again
Magura’s Vyron V3 post is easy to install, has great ergonomics and is built to last, but it's slow when it comes to actuation

component_name: Pirelli's P Zero Road TLR tyre is enticing with a top-level compound, but beware of its narrow sizing
description: Top-level compound yields great grip, but watch out for the weight

component_name: Continental’s Grand Prix TR tyre offers flagship grip and competitive rolling resistance for a good price
description: The Grand Prix TR isn't just a very good tyre for the price – it's a very good tyre, period

component_name: The Michelin Pro5 Competition Line proves you don’t need to spend big for speed
description: Low rolling resistance headlines in this mid-range tyre

component_name: e*thirteen Sidekick hub makes a marked difference to suspension performance
description: The Sidekick hub makes a notable difference to suspension performance, smoothing out the trail with an almost chain-less feel

component_name: Industry Nine Hydra2 Enduro wheels are fast-rolling blingy hoops for those who like buzzy hubs
description: The Hydra2 Enduro wheels feel fast-rolling, accurate and comfy with rapid freehub pick-up

component_name: ENVE's M8 is a smooth-feeling wheelset, but there's a reason you shouldn't buy them
description: Smooth-riding wheels with loads of tech, but they’re so expensive

component_name: Continental's 35mm GP5000 S TR is a sublime road tyre, but there are reasons not to buy
description: There are many reasons to recommend the new 35mm GP5000, but is it worth buying a new bike for?

component_name: Schwalbe's G-One RS Pro is a surprisingly versatile gravel race tyre that goes straight to the top of my list
description: An excellent hardpack-oriented tyre that’s more capable than it looks

component_name: Schwalbe's G-One RX Pro is a light, fast and grippy race-focused gravel tyre
description: A surprisingly speedy tyre for its category, that does everything well

component_name: Continental's Terra Adventure tyres provide seriously impressive grip at the expense of rolling resistance
description: A tough, grippy gravel tyre, but its high rolling resistance means the Terra Adventure is best suited to specific conditions
